odoo.title: ERP
odoo.longDescription: Multi-ERP core with ERP 19 as a first-class connector. Sync models, import catalogs, manage mappings, export profiles, and monitor runs.

help.connection: Save and test the connection to your ERP 19 instance. Without this connection, models, catalogs, and exports cannot run.
help.schema: Sync real models and fields from ERP (for example: sale.order, purchase.order, account.move). This defines which fields are required and writable during export.
help.schemaType: Field data type (char, many2one, float, etc).
help.schemaRequired: If Yes, ERP requires a value to create or update the record.
help.schemaWritable: If Yes, the system can write this field during export.
help.catalog: Import live catalogs from ERP: products/SKUs, partners, and reference data (taxes, currencies, UoM, warehouses, carriers, payment terms). They are used for mapping and validation.
help.sku: Match document SKUs to the correct ERP product. This reduces line-level errors and speeds up approvals.
help.skuSource: SKU detected in the supplier document.
help.skuCompany: Your internal company SKU when applicable.
help.skuProduct: Target ERP product for that line.
help.profiles: An export profile defines which ERP model receives the approved document (sales_order, purchase_order, invoice, shipping, or custom), and which field mapping it uses. You can keep multiple profiles for different flows.
help.profilesFlow: Flow indicates the target document type: sales, purchase, invoice, shipping, or custom.
help.profilesRun: Run export creates the ERP record in draft mode. It does not auto-confirm documents.
help.runs: Execution history for exports. You can see success/failure, created external reference in ERP, and failure reason when present.
help.runsDate: Date and time when the run was started.
help.runsStatus: Final run status: success, failed, pending, etc.
help.runsExternal: ID or name of the record created in ERP.
help.runsDraft: Internal draft used for that export run.

emailIngest.title: Email ingest
emailIngest.description: Receive orders through webhooks, preserve the original email, process PDF/image attachments, and create documents from HTML or text when the order is in the email body.
emailIngest.webhookDescription: Connect this URL to your Email provider Exchange Online mailbox via Email provider notifications. The webhook secret is sent as a query parameter.
emailIngest.mailgunTitle: Set up order receiving with Mailgun
emailIngest.mailgunDescription: Create an email source, copy its webhook, and use it as the Forward destination in a Mailgun route. Every source has its own secret.
emailIngest.mailgunStepDomainText: Add and verify your receiving domain in Mailgun.
emailIngest.microsoftDescription: Use an Exchange Online shared mailbox and Email provider notifications. The system receives the notification, downloads the message, and processes attachments or HTML through the same pipeline.
emailIngest.microsoftAppText: Register an app with the client secret stored as an environment variable.
emailIngest.createFailed: Email source could not be created.
emailIngest.subject: Subject

quality.securityText: Every webhook requires its own secret and sources can restrict senders by email or domain.
quality.idempotencyText: Message-ID prevents duplicates even when the email provider retries the webhook.
quality.adaptersText: The core supports Email provider, Mailgun, Resend, SendGrid, and generic payloads without locking the app to one provider.

onboarding.title: Welcome to DocFlow

hero.title: Financial operations infrastructure for purchase orders.
hero.subtitle: DocFlow turns PDFs, images, and supplier emails into reviewed draft Sales Orders in ERP. Operators stay in control, audit trails stay complete, and manual entry stops owning the day.

items.label: Approved drafts synced on first push

integrations.label: Designed to fit the systems your back office already runs

solutions.title: One pipeline for every way purchase orders arrive.
solutions.description: Upload files, receive orders by email, connect supplier portals, review extracted data, and create ERP drafts from the same controlled workflow.

items.title: Upload purchase orders
items.description: Drop PDFs and images into a queue built for operators who need to scan, filter, and move quickly.

items.title: Capture email orders
items.description: Pull attachments and message context into the same ingestion layer without forwarding files by hand.

items.title: Connect provider portals
items.description: Preserve evidence from supplier portals and route downloaded orders through provider-aware templates.

items.title: Review before ERP
items.description: Compare extraction results with the source document, fix edge cases, and approve only what is ready.

items.title: Create draft Sales Orders
items.description: Push approved lines to ERP as drafts, keeping confirmation and invoicing in your existing ERP process.

teams.title: Built for high-volume order desks.
teams.description: Built for the people who handle real purchase orders all day: operations teams, admins, and systems owners.

items.label: Operations
items.title: Give operators a faster review desk
items.description: A compact view keeps source documents, extracted lines, provider mapping, and approval status in one place.

items.label: Administration
items.title: Control mappings and exceptions
items.description: Provider-aware field and SKU mapping helps teams handle known suppliers without blocking unknown ones.
items.metric: audit trail from source to draft

items.label: Systems
items.title: Keep ERP as the source of truth
items.description: Approved data lands in ERP as a draft Sales Order, so final commercial control stays inside the ERP.

infrastructure.title: Extensible enough for complex order flows.
infrastructure.description: A controlled layer between messy supplier documents and the ERP records your team depends on.

items.title: Integration-ready workflow
items.description: The upload, review, and approval path is structured around clear states so automation can expand without hiding risk.

items.title: Secure boundaries
items.description: Every document, draft, credit event, provider, and mapping is secured and isolated.

items.title: Human approval gate
items.description: AI accelerates extraction, but ERP sync only happens after an operator approves the draft.

resources.title: The pieces teams expect before replacing manual entry.

items.title: Review quality
items.description: Every PO keeps the original source beside extracted lines so mistakes are visible before approval.

items.title: Exception handling
items.description: Missing SKUs, unknown providers, and sync errors surface as concrete work instead of silent retries.

items.title: ERP-native output
items.description: Sales Orders are created as drafts, letting your existing ERP permissions and process finish the job.

cta.title: Turn the next purchase order into a reviewed ERP draft.
cta.subtitle: Start with the workflow your operators already know, then let the system remove the repetitive typing around it.

getting-started.title: First 5 minutes
getting-started.summary: From zero to your first order in ERP in under 5 minutes.
getting-started.body:
## Before you start

You need three things:

1. An **Anthropic API key** (Claude) — without it the AI can't extract.
2. An active **ERP connection** — the app syncs orders there.
3. At least one **provider** registered so the system knows how to interpret the order.

## Step 1 — Connect Anthropic

Go to **Settings → AI** and paste your API key. If you don't have one, see the dedicated Anthropic setup guide.

## Step 2 — Connect ERP

Go to **Settings → ERP** and fill in: XML-RPC URL, database, user, API key. Press *Test* to verify.

## Step 3 — Upload your first order

Go to **Inbox** and drag a purchase-order PDF. In under 30 seconds you'll see the order in **Needs review** state. Open it, verify the fields, and press **Approve**. The order shows up in ERP as a *Sales Order* or *Quotation* (depending on your config).

## Enable Auto-sync (optional)

Once you trust the extractions, flip the **Auto-sync** toggle in the Inbox header: "clean" orders will go straight to ERP without manual review.

dashboard-tour.title: Dashboard tour
dashboard-tour.summary: What each metric and section of the app means.
dashboard-tour.body:
## Dashboard

Summary of the last period: orders processed, success rate, AI spend, recent errors.

## Inbox

Documents in process or waiting for review. The orange badge shows how many need your attention.

## Review

Focused view to approve/reject one by one. Equivalent to opening a document from Inbox.

## Processed

History of approved/rejected orders. Here you see ERP sync status and can manually retry if anything failed.

## Templates

Manage **providers** and **review profiles**. Define how each PDF field maps to ERP.

## Settings

Your ERP connection, API keys, team, credits, and tenant preferences.

anthropic-key.title: Configure Anthropic API key
anthropic-key.summary: How to obtain an Anthropic API key, paste it into DocFlow, and understand the cost.
anthropic-key.body:
## Why Anthropic?

Intake uses **Claude Sonnet 4.6** to extract data from purchase orders. It's the only AI provider supported.

## Get an API key

1. Go to [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com).
2. Create an account or sign in.
3. Under **Settings → API keys**, click **Create key**. Give it a name like `intake-prod`.
4. **Copy the key immediately** — it's shown only once.
5. Add credit under **Billing** (recommended minimum: $20 USD to start).

## Paste it into DocFlow

1. Go to **Settings → AI** in DocFlow.
2. Paste the API key into the field.
3. Click **Test connection** to verify.
4. Click **Save**.

## Approximate cost

A typical 1-3 page purchase order costs between **$0.01 and $0.05** USD in Claude tokens. Complex multi-line invoices may reach $0.10. Actual cost shows up per document (column *AI cost*).

## Common errors

- **401 Unauthorized**: the key is mistyped or was revoked.
- **529 Overloaded**: Anthropic is saturated; the system retries automatically up to 3 times.
- **Insufficient credits**: you need to top up your Anthropic balance.

odoo-setup.title: Configure ERP connection
odoo-setup.summary: Steps to connect your ERP instance via XML-RPC.
odoo-setup.body:
## What you need

- **Base URL**: e.g. `https://erp.yourcompany.com`.
- **Database**: the ERP database name (e.g. `production`).
- **Username**: an ERP user with permissions to create *Sales Orders*.
- **API key**: generated in ERP from *My Profile → Account → API Keys*.

## Generate an ERP API key

1. In ERP, open your profile (top-right corner).
2. Go to the **Account** tab → **API Keys** → **New API Key**.
3. Name it (`intake-integration`) and copy the key.
4. Paste that key into DocFlow — **don't use your password**, it won't work if you have 2FA.

## Test the connection

1. **Settings → ERP** in DocFlow.
2. Fill in the 4 fields.
3. Click **Test connection**.
4. If everything's OK, click **Save**.

## Export mode

Choose how orders are created in ERP:

- **Quotation**: order is created as draft; a human must confirm it in ERP.
- **Sales order**: order is auto-confirmed on sync.

## Common errors

- **Connection refused**: verify the URL (must include `https://`) and that no firewall is blocking.
- **Invalid login/password**: make sure you're using the API key, not the password.
- **Access denied**: the ERP user lacks permissions for `sale.order` or `res.partner`.

email-ingest.title: Email ingestion
email-ingest.summary: Receive orders automatically from Email provider, Mailgun, or Resend.
email-ingest.body:
## What is it?

Intake can read a mailbox and automatically process any PDF attachment received. Ideal when your suppliers email you orders.

## Email provider (Outlook / Email provider)

1. Register an **App** in Azure AD with `Mail.Read` and `Mail.ReadBasic.All` permissions (delegated or application).
2. Generate a **client secret** in the app.
3. Create a **subscription** pointing to `https://<your-domain>/api/email-ingest?source_id=<ID>` with a random `clientState` (save it, you'll need it).
4. In DocFlow **Ingest Hub → Email sources**, create a new source with adapter `microsoft_graph` and paste: tenant ID, client ID, client secret, graph subscription ID, graph client state.

## Mailgun

1. Configure a **route** in Mailgun forwarding to `https://<your-domain>/api/email-ingest?adapter=mailgun&source_id=<ID>`.
2. Copy your Mailgun **signing key** and expose it as env var `MAILGUN_SIGNING_KEY` in your Supabase project.
3. In DocFlow create the source with adapter `mailgun` and the destination email address.

## Resend

1. Configure an **inbound webhook** in Resend pointing to the same endpoint with `adapter=resend`.
2. Copy the **webhook secret** and store it in the source's `webhook_secret` field in DocFlow.

## Verify

Send a test email with a PDF attachment. Within seconds you should see the document appear in **Inbox**.

## Security

Intake rejects webhooks whose timestamp is more than 15 minutes old (replay protection).

browser-scrape.title: Browser scrape connections
browser-scrape.summary: Configure a crawler that downloads orders from supplier web portals.
browser-scrape.body:
## When to use scraping

When a supplier publishes orders on their web portal (no email or API). The crawler periodically logs in, downloads new PDFs, and feeds them to DocFlow.

## Setup

1. **Ingest Hub → Browser connections → New connection**.
2. Fill in: name, login URL, credentials (encrypted with AES-256).
3. Define a **download selector** (CSS or XPath for the "Download PDF" button).
4. Set the **cadence**: hourly, every 4 hours, or daily.

## Diagnostics

Each run is logged at **Ingest Hub → Runs**. If a run fails:

- **Login failed**: the page changed or the password expired.
- **No new orders**: the selector no longer finds new orders (typical when the portal is redesigned).
- **Download error**: the PDF didn't download (could be timeout or dynamic JS).

## Limitations

- Doesn't support CAPTCHAs.
- Doesn't support MFA — use dedicated accounts without 2FA where possible.
- The portal may detect the bot and block; not the most stable option. Email-ingest is better when available.

cleo-webedi.title: Supplier Portal
cleo-webedi.summary: How DocFlow automatically receives 850 Purchase Orders from Marketplace, Retailer B, Retailer C and other EDI partners.
cleo-webedi.body:
## What is Supplier Portal?

Supplier Portal (`portal.example.com`) is the EDI portal large retailers (Marketplace, Retailer B, Retailer C, Retailer D, etc.) use to send **850 Purchase Orders in X12 format**. DocFlow's account is **DocFlow**.

When a new order arrives, Supplier Portal sends a notification email to `orders@example.com` containing an HTML table with Supplier Portal's internal `Message ID`. DocFlow uses that ID to download the PDF directly from the portal and feed it to the AI pipeline.

## Why email as trigger?

A cron-based alternative would poll Supplier Portal for unread messages every N minutes. Problem: if another user opens an order in the portal, Supplier Portal marks it as read and the cron loses it. Using **email as the trigger** guarantees each notification is processed exactly once, regardless of who else accesses the portal.

## End-to-end flow

1. Marketplace/Retailer C/etc. publishes an 850 in Supplier Portal.
2. Supplier Portal sends an email to `orders@example.com` with a metadata table.
3. DocFlow receives the email (Email provider webhook), detects it's a Supplier Portal notification, parses the table.
4. For each row with `Document = 850`, dispatches to the Playwright runner.
5. The runner logs into Supplier Portal, calls `portal.printDocument(messageId)`, downloads the PDF.
6. The PDF goes through the AI pipeline — lines, quantities, prices extracted.
7. It appears in the Inbox as `needs_review`.

## Important filters

- **850s only**: any other message type (855 ack, 856 ASN, 997 functional ack, etc.) is ignored.
- **Notifications no longer appear as HTML docs**: previously the notification email generated a junk HTML document in the Inbox; now it's discarded.
- **Idempotency**: the `cleo_orders` table has unique on `(tenant_id, cleo_message_id)` — no duplicate downloads.

## If something fails

- Open **Ingest Hub → Supplier Portal**.
- Run the **smoke test** — checks env vars, portal connectivity, runner endpoint.
- Failed orders show with a **Retry** button.
- If the account password changes, update `CLEO_PASSWORD` in Vercel.

## Limitations

- Only DocFlow (account ). For more Supplier Portal accounts, multi-tenant changes are needed in `cleo_orders`.
- If Supplier Portal changes its internal JS API (`portal.printDocument`), the runner fails. The smoke test would catch this.
- Playwright cold start on Vercel: ~5-10 s. The runner has `maxDuration=300` so there's plenty of margin.

## Endpoints (audit trail)

- `POST /api/ingest/cleo/process` — runner protected by `INTAKE_CLEO_INTERNAL_TOKEN`.
- `POST /api/ingest/cleo/smoke` — health check.
- `POST /api/ingest/cleo/orders/[id]/retry` — retry a failed order.

provider-templates.title: Provider templates
provider-templates.summary: When to create a provider template and how it's used.
provider-templates.body:
## What is a template?

A **provider** represents who sends you the order (e.g., Home Depot, Retailer D). The **template** defines how to extract and map the document's fields into DocFlow's data model.

## Auto-detection

When you upload a PDF, DocFlow tries to identify the provider via:

1. **Email domain** (if it came in via email-ingest).
2. **Provider name/code** detected in the PDF text.
3. **Fuzzy match** against already-registered providers.

## When to create a template manually

- If detection repeatedly fails for a provider.
- If the provider uses a non-standard format (e.g., dual SKU codes, unusual columns).
- If you want to assign a specific **reseller mapping** (which ERP partner to use as customer).

## Steps

1. **Templates → Create template**.
2. Assign name, code, and email domain (optional).
3. Configure **field annotations**: visually mark on a sample PDF where each field is (PO number, customer, etc).
4. Associate a **reseller mapping** if applicable.

## Field annotations vs Target fields

- **Target fields**: global list of fields ERP needs (e.g., `partner_id`, `client_order_ref`).
- **Field annotations**: visual marks that teach the AI where to find each field on a specific provider's documents.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.summary: Key terms used in DocFlow and what each one means.
glossary.body:
## Review Profile

Configuration per **document type** (PO, invoice, packing slip). Defines which fields are required and how each one is validated before approval.

## Provider Template

Rule per **provider** (Home Depot, etc). Defines how to identify the provider and which `field annotations` to apply to their documents.

## Target Field

A **destination** field in ERP. Examples: `partner_id`, `client_order_ref`, `date_order`, `currency_id`, `name` (line), `product_id`, `product_uom_qty`, `price_unit`. Those marked `required` must be present for auto-sync to fire.

## Reseller Mapping

Maps a **provider** (e.g., Home Depot) to a **specific ERP partner** (e.g., the customer "Retailer D"). Without this, the system doesn't know whose name to create the order under.

## Auto-sync

Per-tenant toggle. When **ON**, "clean" documents (all required fields OK) are auto-approved and synced to ERP without human review.

## Field Annotation

A visual mark per provider that teaches the AI where each field is. Learned when a human corrects an extraction.

## Document states

| State | Means |
|---|---|
| `uploaded` | Just uploaded, queued |
| `processing` | The AI is extracting |
| `needs_review` | Ready for human approval |
| `reviewed` | Approved |
| `rejected` | Rejected |
| `failed_processing` | Extraction failed |
| `archived` | Moved to archive |
| `split` | Was a multi-order PDF; each child is processed separately |

## Order draft (sync) states

| State | Means |
|---|---|
| `none` | No draft |
| `pending` | Approved, waiting to sync |
| `in_progress` | Syncing to ERP right now |
| `synced` | Reached ERP (SO exists) |
| `sync_failed` | ERP rejected the order |

upload-review-approve-sync.title: The flow: Upload → Review → Approve → Sync
upload-review-approve-sync.summary: DocFlow's canonical happy path explained step by step.
upload-review-approve-sync.body:
## 1. Upload

Drag a PDF (or JPG/PNG/WEBP, max 25 MB) into the **Inbox**. Automatic ingestion via email or scraping is also supported.

The document moves through `uploaded` → `processing` (~5-30 seconds).

## 2. Extract (automatic)

Claude Sonnet 4.6 reads the PDF and produces a normalized object: PO number, date, customer, lines with SKU/quantity/price, totals. If multiple orders are detected in one PDF, it splits into children.

## 3. Review

The document moves to `needs_review`. Open it in **Review** and verify:

- Header: PO number, date, customer, currency.
- Shipping/Billing: shipping and billing addresses.
- Lines: SKU, description, quantity, unit price.
- Notes: special instructions.

If something's wrong, edit the field. If you want the AI to learn from the correction, mark a new *field annotation*.

## 4. Approve

Click **Approve**. The document moves to `reviewed` and the ERP sync fires.

## 5. Sync

The order is created in ERP (Quotation or Sales Order based on your config). State moves to `synced` and the SO link appears. If it fails, go to **Processed** and use the manual retry button.

## Auto-sync

If the Auto-sync toggle is on and all gates pass (resolved template, mapped reseller, all required fields present), steps 3-5 happen automatically without human intervention.

auto-sync.title: Auto-sync explained
auto-sync.summary: What the Auto-sync toggle does and when it decides to skip review.
auto-sync.body:
## What does it do?

When on, documents whose extraction is "perfect" are auto-approved and synced to ERP without a human ever opening **Review**. Reduces processing time to ~30 seconds per order.

## When does it fire

Must pass **4 gates**:

1. **Resolved template**: the system identified the provider (`provider_id` is not null).
2. **Reseller mapping**: a provider → ERP partner mapping exists for this tenant.
3. **Complete header**: all `target_fields` with `scope=header` and `required=true` are present in the extraction.
4. **Complete lines**: each line has `name`, `quantity > 0`, and `unit_price` not null (if marked required).

If any gate fails, the document stays in `needs_review` for a human to check.

## Why does a "clean" doc still land in review?

Use the telemetry: every decision emits an `auto_approve` event with `outcome` and `meta.reason`. Query `workflow_events` filtered by `stage=auto_approve` to see the exact reason (`template_not_resolved`, `no_reseller_mapping`, `missing_header_fields`, `incomplete_line_fields`).

## Enable / disable

In the Inbox header, click the **Auto-sync** chip and flip the toggle. Only tenant **owners** can change it (RLS enforces this).

## Documents that never auto-approve

- Orders without unit price on lines (typical for Home Depot school POs).
- New providers without a configured reseller mapping.
- Documents the AI had to split into children: each child evaluates the gates separately.

failed-extraction.title: Documents that fail extraction
failed-extraction.summary: How to diagnose and resolve errors in the AI extraction phase.
failed-extraction.body:
## Symptoms

- The document appears as **Failed** in the Inbox.
- Red banner up top: *"AI provider unavailable"* or similar.
- The document's `last_error` field has the detail.

## Common causes

### 1. Anthropic 529 Overloaded

Claude is saturated. The system retries automatically up to 3 times with backoff (4s, 8s). If all 3 fail, the doc is marked failed with tag `ai_provider_unavailable`.

**Fix**: wait 5-10 minutes and click **Retry All**.

### 2. Corrupt or encrypted PDF

If the PDF is password-protected, the AI can't read it.

**Fix**: decrypt the PDF before uploading (Adobe Acrobat → Remove security).

### 3. Low image quality

Very blurry or rotated scans can produce low-confidence extractions.

**Fix**: rescan at 300 DPI minimum, in correct orientation.

### 4. Invalid API key or no credit

Go to **Settings → AI** and verify the key is active and the Anthropic account has balance.

## Retry

- **Retry All** in the Failed banner: reprocesses all failed Inbox docs.
- **Individual retry**: hover the row → retry button.

## Prevention

If one provider fails repeatedly:

1. Create a **provider template** with precise field annotations.
2. Make sure the PDF format is consistent across orders.

retry-sync.title: Retry ERP sync
retry-sync.summary: Common errors when syncing to ERP and how to resolve them.
retry-sync.body:
## When do I need this?

When an approved order didn't reach ERP. Draft state: `pending` (stuck) or `sync_failed`.

## Manual sync button

On the **Processed** page, hover over any Pending/Failed row → green refresh icon appears. Click → odoo-sync fires again.

Protected by rate limit: max **30 retries per hour per tenant**.

## Common ERP errors

### `Partner not found`

The reseller mapping points to an ERP partner that no longer exists (was deleted).

**Fix**: go to **Templates → Provider** for the affected provider and reconfigure the reseller mapping.

### `Product not found`

A SKU on the order isn't in the ERP catalog.

**Fix**:

1. Sync the ERP catalog (**Settings → ERP → Sync products**).
2. Create a product mapping for that specific SKU.

### `Cross-tenant product violation`

The order references an `odoo_product_id` that doesn't belong to your catalog. This is a security check.

**Fix**: verify the draft doesn't have product IDs from another tenant (rare; usually from manual DB edits).

### `Insufficient stock`

Odoo is configured to reject orders without available stock.

**Fix**: either add stock in ERP, or change export mode to **Quotation** (which doesn't check stock).

## Network errors

- **Timeout**: ERP didn't respond in 60s. Verify the server is up.
- **Connection refused**: URL/port changed. Update **Settings → ERP**.

## When all else fails

Open the document from Processed, verify the extracted data, and manually create the order in ERP. Then mark the document as `archived` to remove it from active flow.

pipeline.title: Processing pipeline
pipeline.summary: Full interactive flow: from a PDF to an ERP order in 6 stages.

tech-stack.title: Tech stack
tech-stack.summary: Anthropic, Supabase, Vercel, and ERP: what each component contributes.

header.title: Processing pipeline
header.subtitle: From a PDF to an ERP order. Click each stage to see exactly what happens, which tool does it, and what comes out the other side.

autosync.label: Simulate Auto-sync
autosync.onDesc: Clean docs go straight to ERP without human review
autosync.offDesc: Every document goes through manual review in the Inbox

labels.lifecycleNote: + sync states in order_drafts: none → pending → in_progress → synced (or sync_failed)

upload.label: Upload
upload.what: The document arrives at DocFlow via one of three channels: manual upload from the Inbox, email attachment (email-ingest), or automatic download from a supplier portal (browser scrape).
upload.step1: File is validated: MIME type (PDF/JPG/PNG/WEBP) and max 25 MB size
upload.step2: Uploaded to Supabase Storage 'documents' bucket at {tenant}/{year-month}/{uuid}.pdf
upload.step3: Row is created in documents table with state = 'uploaded'
upload.step4: Edge Function 'ingest' is triggered with document_id and tenant_id

split.label: Split (optional)
split.what: If the PDF contains multiple purchase orders, a lightweight model (Haiku) detects page boundaries between orders and splits the file into independent children.
split.step1: Claude Haiku scans the full PDF for PO headers, order numbers, and customer changes
split.step2: If > 1 order is detected, each page range is extracted with pdf-lib and child PDFs are uploaded to Storage
split.step3: Parent document moves to state = 'split' and disappears from Inbox
split.step4: Each child starts its own pipeline from scratch

extract.label: AI Extraction
extract.what: Claude Sonnet 4.6 receives the PDF as an image (multimodal vision) and produces a normalized JSON object with every order field. Retries up to 3 times if Anthropic returns 529 (overload).
extract.step1: PDF is converted to base64 and sent to the Anthropic API as a document block
extract.step2: Prompt instructs Claude to produce JSON with: PO number, date, customer, address, lines (SKU, description, quantity, price), totals
extract.step3: Response is validated by a Zod schema: ISO dates, 3-char currency code, numeric totals
extract.step4: If confidence is low, issues are evaluated and we decide if the doc qualifies for auto-approve
extract.fail: If all 3 retries fail → documents.state = 'failed_processing' with last_error = 'ai_provider_unavailable'

template.label: Provider detection
template.what: The system identifies the provider via three cascading methods. Critical step for auto-sync: without a resolved provider, auto-approve fails.
template.step1: 1. Domain match: if it came via email, the sender's domain is checked against providers.email_domains
template.step2: 2. Code match: the slugified provider code detected in the text vs. providers.code
template.step3: 3. Fuzzy name match: if the AI-extracted name overlaps ≥ 4 characters with a registered provider
template.step4: If resolved, the matching review_profile for the document type is loaded

review.label: Review
review.what: Document arrives at 'needs_review'. If Auto-sync is ON and all gates pass, it's auto-approved. Otherwise it waits in the Inbox for human review.
review.step1: Gate 1: template resolved (provider_id is not null)
review.step2: Gate 2: reseller mapping exists (provider → ERP partner)
review.step3: Gate 3: all required header target_fields are present
review.step4: Gate 4: every line has name, quantity > 0, and price_unit if required
review.step5: If all 4 gates pass → auto-approve. Otherwise → human reviews in the UI

sync.label: Sync to ERP
sync.what: The odoo-sync Edge Function calls ERP's XML-RPC API to create the sale. It looks up or creates the partner, maps products by SKU, and returns the SO number.
sync.step1: Resolves the ERP partner: searches by name/email, creates if missing (per contact_settings)
sync.step2: For each line, resolves odoo_product_id: first via provider_product_mappings, then by catalog code search
sync.step3: Calls sale.order.create() via XML-RPC with all fields
sync.step5: Stores odoo_so_id and odoo_so_name in order_drafts and marks sync_state = 'synced'
sync.fail: sync_state = 'sync_failed' with last_sync_error. Manual retry from Processed.

header.title: Tech stack
header.subtitle: DocFlow is built on top-tier cloud infrastructure. Each layer has a clear responsibility, and together they form a robust and scalable pipeline.

anthropic.name: Anthropic Claude
anthropic.c1Desc: Claude Sonnet 4.6 receives the PDF as a base64 image (vision + documents) and extracts every PO field in one call. No OCR pre-step needed.
anthropic.c2Desc: The model produces JSON with a strict schema: po_number, ISO date, 3-char currency, lines with SKU/qty/price. Zod validates the output.
anthropic.c3Desc: Claude Haiku detects how many orders are in a multi-order PDF and returns page ranges for the split. Cheaper model = minimal cost for this task.
anthropic.c4Desc: Each response includes a confidence field (0–1). The system evaluates issues (malformed dates, totals that don't add up) and decides whether the doc qualifies for auto-approve.

supabase.name: Supabase
supabase.tagline: Backend-as-a-service — database, auth, storage, and Edge Functions
supabase.c1Desc: All tables have Row Level Security. Users only see data from their tenant. Policies guarantee isolation without extra backend logic.
supabase.c2Desc: ai-process, ingest, odoo-sync, and email-ingest run as serverless functions on Supabase's edge. No servers to maintain — they scale automatically.
supabase.c3Desc: The Inbox uses Supabase Realtime to update the document list without polling. When a doc moves to 'needs_review', the user sees it in < 1 second.
supabase.c4Desc: Private 'documents' bucket with storage policies per tenant_id. No user can read another tenant's files by guessing the path.
supabase.c5Desc: Auth with magic links and email/password. JWTs include tenant claims. The service role key never reaches the client.

vercel.name: Vercel + Next.js 16
vercel.tagline: Frontend and API routes with React's most modern framework
vercel.c1Desc: Pages are Server Components by default. Only interactive components (Inbox table, review workspace) are Client Components. Less JS shipped to the browser.
vercel.c2Desc: Every /api/* route is a serverless function running on Vercel Edge or Node.js as needed. Auth, tenant authorization, and input validation on every endpoint.
vercel.c3Desc: Spanish and English UI. Routes use the /[locale]/ prefix. Help Center articles are 100% translated in both languages.
vercel.c4Desc: Design system with oklch CSS variables. Accessible Radix UI components. Dark sidebar, light content area, full dark mode support.
vercel.c5Desc: Heavy pages (Templates, Settings) use Suspense boundaries to show skeleton loaders while Supabase queries resolve.

odoo.name: ERP 19
odoo.tagline: Destination ERP — where Sales Orders live
odoo.c1Desc: DocFlow calls ERP via XML-RPC (ERP's native protocol). API key authentication. Creates sale.order, res.partner, and attaches ir.attachment with the original PDF.
odoo.c2Desc: The catalog sync downloads product.product from ERP and stores them in odoo_products. PO SKUs are mapped against this catalog to resolve odoo_product_id.
odoo.c3Desc: Quotation: the order remains in draft, a human confirms it in ERP. Sales Order: auto-confirmed on sync. Configurable per tenant.
odoo.c4Desc: Reseller mapping allows a Home Depot order to create the SO in the correct ERP company with the correct partner, even in multi-company setups.

indexFeatured.pipelineDesc: Explore every processing stage — click and see exactly what the system does.
indexFeatured.techStackDesc: Anthropic, Supabase, Vercel, and ERP — what each component contributes and how they integrate.

notificationsSettings.pageSubtitle: Central hub to configure every automatic email DocFlow sends — recipients, templates, and schedules. Emails go out from orders@example.com via Email provider.

integrationsDiagnostics.title: Integrations diagnostics
integrationsDiagnostics.subtitle: Real-time status of the 4 ingest sources

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.pageSubtitle: {count, plural, =0 {All read} one {# unread} other {# unread}}

odooCatalogSync.title: Sync from ERP
odooCatalogSync.subtitle: Pick what to pull — each block refreshes a different cache layer

products.title: Products
products.summary: Active product catalog from ERP (SKU, code, barcode, UoM)
products.detail2: Auto-deactivates entries ERP no longer returns (reaper)
products.detail3: Nightly cron runs at 3am local time

catalog.title: Customers & references
catalog.summary: Partners (companies), taxes, currencies, payment terms, warehouses, carriers, sales teams, UoMs
catalog.detail1: Needed to resolve the customer when approving an order
catalog.detail2: Only top-level partners (companies or individuals without a parent)
catalog.detail3: Refreshes currencies, taxes, and warehouses for ERP selects

schema.title: Model schema
schema.summary: Field and type metadata for key ERP models (sale.order, res.partner, etc.)
schema.detail1: Only needed for advanced template creation or field-type inspection
schema.detail2: Does not affect product or order syncs
schema.detail3: Changes infrequently — no need to run it often

all.title: Sync everything
all.summary: Runs all 3 syncs in order: customers/references → products → schema
all.detail1: Takes as long as the three combined (customers is usually the slowest)
all.detail2: If one fails, earlier ones stay applied; you can retry the failed one
all.detail3: Uses ERP API quota — avoid during peak activity hours

emailInbound.title: Email Inbound
emailInbound.subtitle: Historical view of all emails received at orders@example.com (Email provider). Read-only.
emailInbound.messages: {count, plural, =0 {No messages} one {# message} other {# messages}}
